comment_text.sn22.100:2.1: Said by the commentary to be a large painting taken from place to place by certain brahmin heretics in their “wanderings” and exhibited to illustrate kamma and its results. This suggests the sense of “conduct” (caraṇa), although the commentary does not make this connection. Perhaps, too, there is a pun on the idea of “wandering mind”. | The commentary says the brahmins were of the Sāṁkhya school, but this is anachronistic as that school arose much later.
comment_text.sn22.100:2.3: The text plays on three meanings of citta: “mind”, “painting”, and “diversity”, as well as the past participle cittita, “thought up”, “devised”.

root_text.sn22.100:1.3: Pubbā koṭi na paññāyati avijjānīvaraṇānaṁ sattānaṁ taṇhāsaṁyojanānaṁ sandhāvataṁ saṁsarataṁ.
root_text.sn22.100:1.4: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, sā gaddulabaddho daḷhe khīle vā thambhe vā upanibaddho. So gacchati cepi tameva khīlaṁ vā thambhaṁ vā upagacchati; tiṭṭhati cepi tameva khīlaṁ vā thambhaṁ vā upatiṭṭhati; nisīdati cepi tameva khīlaṁ vā thambhaṁ vā upanisīdati; nipajjati cepi tameva khīlaṁ vā thambhaṁ vā upanipajjati.
root_text.sn22.100:1.5: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, assutavā puthujjano rūpaṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassati.
root_text.sn22.100:1.9: viññāṇaṁ ‘etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā’ti samanupassati.
root_text.sn22.100:1.10: So gacchati cepi ime pañcupādānakkhandhe upagacchati;
root_text.sn22.100:1.14: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, abhikkhaṇaṁ sakaṁ cittaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ:
root_text.sn22.100:2.1: Diṭṭhaṁ vo, bhikkhave, caraṇaṁ nāma cittan”ti?
root_text.sn22.100:2.3: “Tampi kho, bhikkhave, caraṇaṁ nāma cittaṁ citteneva cittitaṁ. Tenapi kho, bhikkhave, caraṇena cittena cittaññeva cittataraṁ.
root_text.sn22.100:2.4: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, abhikkhaṇaṁ sakaṁ cittaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ:
root_text.sn22.100:3.1: Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekanikāyampi samanupassāmi evaṁ cittaṁ yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, tiracchānagatā pāṇā.
root_text.sn22.100:3.2: tepi kho, bhikkhave, tiracchānagatā pāṇā citteneva cittitā, tehipi kho, bhikkhave, tiracchānagatehi pāṇehi cittaññeva cittataraṁ.
root_text.sn22.100:3.3: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, abhikkhaṇaṁ sakaṁ cittaṁ paccavekkhitabbaṁ:
root_text.sn22.100:4.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, rajako vā cittakārako vā rajanāya vā lākhāya vā haliddiyā vā nīliyā vā mañjiṭṭhāya vā suparimaṭṭhe phalake vā bhittiyā vā dussapaṭṭe vā itthirūpaṁ vā purisarūpaṁ vā abhinimmineyya sabbaṅgapaccaṅgiṁ;
root_text.sn22.100:4.2: evameva kho, bhikkhave, assutavā puthujjano rūpaññeva abhinibbattento abhinibbatteti, vedanaññeva …pe… saññaññeva … saṅkhāreyeva … viññāṇaññeva abhinibbattento abhinibbatteti.

translation_text.sn22.100:1.2: “Mendicants, this transmigration has no known beginning.
translation_text.sn22.100:1.3: No first point is found of sentient beings roaming and transmigrating, shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving.
translation_text.sn22.100:1.4: Suppose a hound on a leash was tethered to a strong post or pillar. Whether walking, standing, sitting, or lying down, it stays right beside that post or pillar.
translation_text.sn22.100:1.5: In the same way, an unlearned ordinary person regards form like this: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self.’
translation_text.sn22.100:1.9: consciousness like this: ‘This is mine, I am this, this is my self.’
translation_text.sn22.100:1.10: When walking, they walk right beside the five grasping aggregates.
translation_text.sn22.100:1.13: lying down, they lie down right beside the five grasping aggregates.
translation_text.sn22.100:1.14: So you should regularly check your own mind:
translation_text.sn22.100:1.15: ‘For a long time this mind has been corrupted by greed, hate, and delusion.’
translation_text.sn22.100:1.16: Sentient beings are corrupted because the mind is corrupted.
translation_text.sn22.100:1.17: Sentient beings are purified because the mind is purified.
translation_text.sn22.100:2.1: Mendicants, have you seen the painting named ‘Conduct’?”
translation_text.sn22.100:2.3: “That painting was devised by the mind, but the mind is even more diverse than that.
translation_text.sn22.100:2.4: So you should regularly check your own mind:
translation_text.sn22.100:2.5: ‘For a long time this mind has been corrupted by greed, hate, and delusion.’
translation_text.sn22.100:2.6: Sentient beings are corrupted because the mind is corrupted.
translation_text.sn22.100:2.7: Sentient beings are purified because the mind is purified.
translation_text.sn22.100:3.1: I don’t see any other order of beings as elaborate as the animal realm.
translation_text.sn22.100:3.2: The creatures in the animal realm were devised by the mind, but the mind is even more diverse than that.
translation_text.sn22.100:3.3: So you should regularly check your own mind:
translation_text.sn22.100:3.4: ‘For a long time this mind has been corrupted by greed, hate, and delusion.’
translation_text.sn22.100:3.5: Sentient beings are corrupted because the mind is corrupted.
translation_text.sn22.100:3.6: Sentient beings are purified because the mind is purified.
translation_text.sn22.100:4.1: Suppose an artist or painter had some dye, red lac, turmeric, indigo, or rose madder. And on a polished plank or a wall or a canvas they’d create the form of a woman or a man, whole in its major and minor limbs.
translation_text.sn22.100:4.2: In the same way, when an unlearned ordinary person generates a future life, all they generate is form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.
translation_text.sn22.100:4.12: They understand: ‘… there is nothing further for this place.’”
